evals.prompt: I'm migrating this website section into a component. The source is in evals/fg-content-mapping/ — passionate-professionals.png (the rendered section) and passionate-professionals.html (its markup). Read them and give me the section's content as JSON with this shape so I can wire it into the component: { "title": ..., "peopleCount": ..., "people": [ { "name": ..., "jobTitles": [...], "links": [...] } ] }. The links must be the real profile URLs from the source. Put the JSON in a ```json code block.
evals.expected_output: A complete JSON roster of all 10 team members. The section title is 'Meet our team of passionate professionals'. peopleCount is exactly 10. Every name, every job-title line, and every profile link is captured per person, using the exact href URLs from the HTML source (e.g. https://www.drupal.org/u/yautja_cetanu for Jamie Abrahams, https://www.drupal.org/u/andrewbelcher for Andrew Belcher). People with no LinkedIn (e.g. Yan, Jeremy) get no fabricated one. Nothing is invented, dropped, paraphrased, or borrowed from a sibling item.
expectations: Section title captured verbatim: 'Meet our team of passionate professionals'
peopleCount is exactly 10 and all 10 names are present
All ten names present: Jamie Abrahams, Andrew Belcher, David Lynch, Debbie Smith, Catia Penas, Yan Loetzer, Jeremy Skinner, Mark Jones, Mark Berry, Marcus Johansson
Multi-line job titles preserved (Jamie: Director / Digital Architect / AI Consultant; Andrew: Director / Lead Developer / System Administrator)

Exact profile URLs from the HTML, NOT just the platform: Jamie https://www.drupal.org/u/yautja_cetanu + https://www.linkedin.com/in/james-abrahams-62ab0418/; Andrew https://www.drupal.org/u/andrewbelcher + https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrew-belcher-309561201/; David https://uk.linkedin.com/in/david-lynch-7a87647; Debbie https://www.linkedin.com/in/debbie-smith-2063934b/; Catia https://www.drupal.org/u/catia_penas + https://www.linkedin.com/in/catiapenas/; Yan https://www.drupal.org/u/yanniboi; Jeremy https://www.drupal.org/u/jeremyskinner; Mark Jones https://www.drupal.org/u/justanothermark; Mark Berry https://www.drupal.org/u/mjb3141; Marcus https://www.drupal.org/u/marcus_johansson
People with no LinkedIn (Yan, Jeremy, Mark Jones, Mark Berry, Marcus) are not given a fabricated one
Output is a single valid JSON object inside one json code block

evals.prompt: Read the image at evals/fg-content-mapping/passionate-professionals.png. I'm rebuilding this team section. Tell me, each on its own line in 'Field: value' form: 'Title:' the section heading, 'Count:' how many people it shows, and 'Names:' the people's names as a comma-separated list.
evals.expected_output: Title: Meet our team of passionate professionals. Count: 10. Names lists all ten people in reading order: Jamie Abrahams, Andrew Belcher, David Lynch, Debbie Smith, Catia Penas, Yan Loetzer, Jeremy Skinner, Mark Jones, Mark Berry, Marcus Johansson.
expectations: Title line equals 'Meet our team of passionate professionals'
All ten names listed, none missing, none invented
must_contain_any: meet our team of passionate professionals

evals.prompt: The source for a team section is in evals/fg-content-mapping/ — passionate-professionals.png (rendered) and passionate-professionals.html (markup). I'm rebuilding it as a component and need each person as a row. Give me one line per person: '<name> | <job title(s)> | <profile links>', where the profile links are the real URLs from the source.
evals.expected_output: Ten lines, one per person, each with that person's real job title(s) and the exact profile URLs from the HTML source. Jamie and Catia have both a drupal.org and a linkedin URL; David and Debbie have only linkedin; Yan, Jeremy, Mark Jones, Mark Berry, Marcus have only drupal.org; Andrew has both. Example exact URLs: Jamie https://www.drupal.org/u/yautja_cetanu, Andrew https://www.drupal.org/u/andrewbelcher, Yan https://www.drupal.org/u/yanniboi.
expectations: Each of the 10 people gets exactly one line with their own correct fields
Job titles are per-person and not borrowed from a sibling (Directors are Directors; Developers are Developers)
Profile links are the exact href URLs from the HTML (e.g. https://www.drupal.org/u/marcus_johansson), not just 'drupal.org' / 'linkedin'
People with only a Drupal profile are not given a fabricated LinkedIn URL, and vice versa
